target.title: Add COE root-cause skill
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `coe-root-cause` to the ClawHub public corpus as an installable Correction of Error root-cause analysis skill.

The skill is based on Amazon's rigorous Correction of Error (COE) practice: a blameless, evidence-backed post-incident loop that focuses on root cause, corrective actions, owner accountability, and durable prevention.

## Why

This is proposed as a curated public-corpus fixture, not as an owner-published community skill. The purpose is to include one broadly reusable, tool-agnostic COE skill in the committed public corpus used for local dev seeding, corpus validation, and install-surface QA.

Because this route adds installable skill content through `fixtures/public-corpus`, it should merge only if maintainers approve this as a curated corpus exception to the normal owner-scoped `clawhub skill publish` path.

## What changed

- Adds one public corpus skill row for `coe-root-cause`.
- Adds a readable source fixture at `fixtures/public-corpus/skills/coe-root-cause/SKILL.md` so reviewers can inspect the actual skill content.
- Keeps the readable fixture and public corpus JSONL row synchronized with a regression test.
- Documents the curated public-corpus skill exception in `specs/dev-seeding.md`.
- Updates `AGENTS.md` so direct skill additions remain rejected unless maintainers explicitly approve that curated corpus exception.
- Updates the public corpus manifest to clarify that dummy seeded ownership is fixture-local, not real publisher provenance.
- Clarifies the skill-library boundary: `coe-root-cause` handles formal post-failure COE reports, while debugging, review, retrospective, and skill-creation skills keep their narrower roles.
- Keeps the skill tool-agnostic and public-safe.
- Explicitly asks agents to redact secrets, tokens, PII, customer data, and private workspace details.
- Clarifies that report-only requests should propose corrective actions unless the user asks to apply them.
- Adapts the Amazon/AWS COE pattern for agent workflow failures, false success, data loss, missed work, and brittle automation.

## Behavioural proof

- [x] Behavioural proof included.

`bun run validate:public-corpus`

```text
{
  "ok": true,
  "fixture": "fixtures/public-corpus/corpus.jsonl",
  "rowCount": 1251,
  "skillCount": 1001,
  "pluginCount": 250
}
```

`bun test scripts/public-corpus/validate.test.ts scripts/public-corpus/seed-public-corpus.test.ts`

```text
11 pass
0 fail
24 expect() calls
Ran 11 tests across 2 files.
```

The focused test run includes:

- public corpus fixture validation
- rejection of owner identity fields and raw Convex ids
- rejection of secret-like text and raw local absolute paths
- readable `coe-root-cause/SKILL.md` fixture sync with the JSONL corpus row
- public corpus seed runner retry behavior

`bun run ci:static`

```text
peer ok: @auth/core 0.37.4 satisfies @convex-dev/auth (^0.37.0)
No vulnerabilities found
All matched files use the correct format.
oxlint completed successfully
deadcode:files, deadcode:dependencies, and deadcode:exports completed successfully
```

## Security / trust impact

- [x] Security/trust impact explained.

This PR proposes a curated public-corpus exception. It does not exercise the normal owner-scoped publish flow, so maintainer approval is required before merge.

Mitigations included in the patch:

- the repo policy now keeps direct skill additions rejected unless maintainers explicitly approve the curated public-corpus exception in `specs/dev-seeding.md`
- the dev-seeding spec now defines when curated corpus skill exceptions are allowed and what evidence is required before merge
- the public corpus manifest now clarifies that dummy seeded ownership is fixture-local and not publisher provenance
- the public corpus validator rejects owner identity fields, raw Convex ids, secret-like text, and raw local absolute paths
- the added skill content is readable in the PR, not hidden only inside a JSONL row
- the readable fixture and JSONL row are locked together by test coverage
- the skill instructs agents to redact secrets, tokens, PII, customer data, and private workspace details
- the PR body cites the public sources used to adapt the COE process

## Data / deploy impact

- [x] Data/deploy impact explained.

This changes the committed local public corpus fixture. It increases the fixture count from 1250 to 1251 total rows and from 1000 to 1001 skill rows. It does not change production deploy code.

## Sources

- AWS Cloud Operations Blog: [Why you should develop a Correction of Error (COE)](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/mt/why-you-should-develop-a-correction-of-error-coe/)
- AWS Well-Architected Framework: [REL12-BP02 Perform post-incident analysis](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/rel_testing_resiliency_rca_resiliency.html)

## Verification

- [x] `bun run validate:public-corpus`
- [x] `bun test scripts/public-corpus/validate.test.ts scripts/public-corpus/seed-public-corpus.test.ts`
- [x] `bun run ci:static`
- [x] Readable-fixture sync check for `coe-root-cause`
- [x] Public/private leakage scan on the added diff
target.author: ghitafilali
